jokes.text: My wife dragged me there. She had these tickets... It's a little embarrassing to me.
jokes.setup: Larry is embarrassed about going to a U2 concert
jokes.punchline: The admission that being at a rock concert is embarrassing to him
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry character moment - social anxiety about being seen as uncool while being uncool about it

jokes.text: I don't like the idea that everything I do people are finding out about through my housekeeper, that's all.
jokes.setup: Larry's privacy concerns
jokes.punchline: The paranoid specificity of being surveilled by his own housekeeper
jokes.explanation: Great setup for the episode's running theme, perfectly captures Larry's neurotic control issues

jokes.text: I got a pubic hair.
jokes.setup: Larry has something stuck in his throat
jokes.punchline: The shocking revelation of what it is
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - economy of language with maximum awkwardness impact

jokes.text: That's not that unusual. I got people in here all the time with stuff stuck in their throat.
jokes.setup: The shocking pubic hair revelation
jokes.punchline: Doctor's casual, professional response to something deeply uncomfortable
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan that normalizes the absurd - great straight-man comedy

jokes.text: Generally, between you and me, it's not something I do that often...
jokes.setup: Larry trying to explain how he got a pubic hair in his throat
jokes.punchline: The implication while trying to be discrete
jokes.explanation: Awkward oversharing that's perfectly in character for Larry

jokes.text: Whatever happened, stay away from that for a couple of days.
jokes.setup: Doctor's medical advice about the pubic hair situation
jokes.punchline: The euphemistic medical recommendation
jokes.explanation: Good professional euphemism that maintains the awkwardness

jokes.text: Rock on.
jokes.setup: Doctor saying goodbye after the awkward appointment
jokes.punchline: Ironic reference back to the U2 concert Larry was embarrassed about
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that ties the cold open together

jokes.text: I'm a Jew. To have a tree in the house, it's bad luck. My guy might not... may think I'm switching or something.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his Christmas tree anxiety
jokes.punchline: The idea that God might think he's converting based on holiday decorations
jokes.explanation: Excellent personification of God as 'my guy' with neurotic overthinking

jokes.text: What should I give... ah, you don't know...
jokes.setup: Larry about to ask Cheryl about Christmas tipping
jokes.punchline: Realizing mid-sentence that she wouldn't know either
jokes.explanation: Small moment of Larry's social cluelessness

jokes.text: Could you please just not tell them where I am? Even the doctor's office called up, and you told them I went to a U2 concert, you know?
jokes.setup: Larry addressing the privacy issue from the opening
jokes.punchline: The absurd specificity of the U2 concert leak
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that maintains the episode's throughline

jokes.text: Mr. Larry not home. Okay, okay.
jokes.setup: Dora repeating Larry's instructions
jokes.punchline: Her stilted English making the lie sound rehearsed
jokes.explanation: Mild humor in the language barrier and obvious lie rehearsal

jokes.text: You can use the 'tú' form with me. 'Usted's' a little formal for us.
jokes.setup: Larry giving Carlos a Christmas tip
jokes.punchline: Larry's awkward attempt at Spanish intimacy with his gardener
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment of misguided cultural sensitivity

jokes.text: Feliz navidad... a tú.
jokes.setup: Carlos responding to Larry's Spanish lesson
jokes.punchline: The grammatically incorrect 'a tú' showing he's just humoring Larry
jokes.explanation: Subtle humor in Carlos playing along with bad Spanish grammar

jokes.text: Hey, asshole, I know his name.
jokes.setup: Jeff questioning if Larry knows Bono's name
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate aggressive defensiveness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry overreaction to a minor challenge

jokes.text: I don't know if it's 'Sonny Bo-no,' or Bon-no.
jokes.setup: Larry claiming to know Bono's name
jokes.punchline: Revealing he doesn't know how to pronounce it and comparing to Sonny Bono
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's pseudo-knowledge

jokes.text: Because you spelled it, it's a draw. I don't owe you anything.
jokes.setup: Larry spelling Bono's name instead of pronouncing it
jokes.punchline: Jeff finding a technicality to avoid paying the bet
jokes.explanation: Decent technicality joke showing Jeff's cheapness

jokes.text: Nothing worse than Jews with trees.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Larry getting a Christmas tree
jokes.punchline: Blunt statement about Jewish people celebrating Christmas
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical observation with uncomfortable truth

jokes.text: They can't let them have their holiday. We have to horn in on their holiday.
jokes.setup: Jews with Christmas trees
jokes.punchline: The idea of Jews 'horning in' on Christmas
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the Christmas anxiety theme

jokes.text: Hey, 10 p.m. is the cut-off for platonic friends. After 10 p.m., it gets weird.
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining his late-night phone call lie
jokes.punchline: The specific time-based rule for platonic relationships
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational comedy with a specific, quotable rule

jokes.text: I was calling my housekeeper Dora... She was gonna quit, it was a whole huge thing.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his 2 AM phone call
jokes.punchline: The absurd story about calling his housekeeper at 2 AM about quitting
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of the lie but feels a bit forced

jokes.text: You're lying, or you're lying. One of you two assholes is lying, I just don't know which one.
jokes.setup: Susie confronting Larry and Jeff about their story
jokes.punchline: Susie's blunt assessment that someone is definitely lying
jokes.explanation: Good Susie moment with her characteristic directness

jokes.text: I tipped him twice.
jokes.setup: Larry realizing something about his Christmas tipping
jokes.punchline: The admission that he accidentally double-tipped someone
jokes.explanation: Sets up the tipping confusion plot nicely

jokes.text: 'Cause he makes twice as much money. What do you mean, why?
jokes.setup: Jeff asking why the waiter wouldn't mention being tipped twice
jokes.punchline: Larry's obvious explanation about the waiter's motivation
jokes.explanation: Logical Larry moment showing his cynical worldview

jokes.text: Do you want your first tip back?
jokes.setup: Waiter denying he got tipped twice
jokes.punchline: Larry's aggressive negotiation tactic
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Larry's combative problem-solving

jokes.text: You know what? I think I'm gonna give her my grandfather's tallis.
jokes.setup: Asked what he's getting Cheryl for Christmas
jokes.punchline: Giving his Christian wife a Jewish prayer shawl for Christmas
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry obliviousness to cultural inappropriateness

jokes.text: Grape stem.
jokes.setup: Someone asking about Larry's throat clearing
jokes.punchline: Larry lying about what's stuck in his throat
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Larry's social lie to avoid embarrassment

jokes.text: He's lying, like the waiter.
jokes.setup: Carlos being accused of lying about the tip amount
jokes.punchline: Larry connecting it to his earlier waiter experience
jokes.explanation: Solid callback showing Larry's paranoid pattern recognition

jokes.text: There's a little kind of thing you do at the end... you flip them over, the arms, they get, like, flipped over like a little penguin.
jokes.setup: Larry complimenting Dora's folding skills
jokes.punchline: His overly detailed description of t-shirt folding technique
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe moment with the penguin comparison being oddly specific

jokes.text: I am never in that bathroom looking at cardboard. As soon as it runs out, you've got the new roll.
jokes.setup: Larry praising Dora's toilet paper management
jokes.punchline: The graphic description of never seeing the empty cardboard roll
jokes.explanation: Decent observational moment about household management

jokes.text: I'm never running out of there with my pants at my ankles screaming, 'More toilet paper!'
jokes.setup: Praising toilet paper management
jokes.punchline: The vivid physical image of panic over toilet paper
jokes.explanation: Great visual image that's highly quotable and specific to Larry's neuroses

jokes.text: Your sponge replacement's not so hot... if I can speak frankly.
jokes.setup: Larry giving Dora feedback on her work
jokes.punchline: Critiquing her sponge replacement after all the praise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - can't help but criticize even while apologizing

jokes.text: Christmas.
jokes.setup: Larry asking what the Spanish conversation was about
jokes.punchline: Dora's simple lie to cover discussing Larry with the waiter
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy in a believable lie

jokes.text: All right. I made a mistake, I'm sorry, I apologize. You sure did. I apologize profusely to both of you.
jokes.setup: Jeff realizing Dora is actually Larry's housekeeper
jokes.punchline: Jeff's rapid-fire apology backpedaling
jokes.explanation: Decent moment of Jeff's embarrassment and overcompensation

jokes.text: I don't know why Cheryl doesn't like you. I'm sure it has nothing to do with your work.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to be nice to Dora
jokes.punchline: Accidentally revealing that Cheryl doesn't like Dora
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment of Jeff making things worse while trying to help

jokes.text: What happened to the cookies? The cookies from last night?
jokes.setup: Cheryl looking for cookies
jokes.punchline: Setting up the major disaster Larry is about to reveal
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the major punchline to come

jokes.text: You ate the baby Jesus and his mother Mary.
jokes.setup: Larry ate the cookies
jokes.punchline: The horrifying revelation of what the cookies represented
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect horrified delivery and dark religious comedy

jokes.text: I thought he was a monkey.
jokes.setup: Being accused of eating baby Jesus
jokes.punchline: Larry's defense that he thought Jesus was a zoo animal
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry moment - blasphemous, absurd, perfectly in character. Cultural touchstone level quote.

jokes.text: The Son of God was a monkey?
jokes.setup: Larry's monkey explanation
jokes.punchline: Becky's horrified religious reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man reaction to escalate the blasphemy

jokes.text: I thought that was all part of the zoo.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he ate the nativity scene
jokes.punchline: Larry's continued commitment to the zoo misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Larry's obliviousness to religious symbols

jokes.text: Why would we have a zoo on Christmas Day?!
jokes.setup: Larry's zoo explanation
jokes.punchline: Cheryl pointing out the logical flaw
jokes.explanation: Good logical counterpoint to Larry's absurd reasoning

jokes.text: You swallowed our Lord and Savior.
jokes.setup: The eaten Jesus revelation
jokes.punchline: The ultimate religious horror statement
jokes.explanation: Perfect religious horror phrasing that escalates the blasphemy

jokes.text: You lying piece of shit! Out of my house! And wait till I get my hands on that goddamn Larry David, the four-eyed fuck. I'll kill him!
jokes.setup: Susie discovering the truth about Jeff's lie
jokes.punchline: Susie's volcanic eruption of profanity directed at Larry
jokes.explanation: Perfect Susie explosion with 'four-eyed fuck' being particularly creative

jokes.text: We had one, but I ate it.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they need a nativity scene
jokes.punchline: Larry's casual admission to strangers
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan callback with great economy

jokes.setup: Joseph being asked what would be fair for the nativity performance
jokes.punchline: The absurdly high price for amateur Christmas actors
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how Larry gets taken advantage of

jokes.text: It's not junk, but we do put it in the trailer.
jokes.setup: Larry calling the nativity set 'junk'
jokes.punchline: Joseph's polite but firm correction
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment showing Joseph's religious sensitivity

jokes.text: And around 4th of July we do hobo revolutionaries... And sometimes in November we'll do an equinox.
jokes.setup: Joseph explaining their other performances
jokes.punchline: Bizarre combinations of holidays and themes
jokes.explanation: Absurd holiday performance combinations. 'Hobo revolutionaries' is brilliantly random.

jokes.text: I got a pubic hair stuck in my throat.
jokes.setup: Joseph asking if Larry is okay
jokes.punchline: Larry bringing up his intimate problem to a religious performer
jokes.explanation: Good callback that's inappropriate for the religious context

jokes.text: That Mary, by the way, has quite the bod.
jokes.setup: Larry talking to Joseph about his throat problem
jokes.punchline: Larry sexualizing the Virgin Mary actress
jokes.explanation: Excellent blasphemous moment that's perfectly inappropriate and Larry-like

jokes.text: Kill him, Joseph, kill him!
jokes.setup: Joseph and Larry fighting
jokes.punchline: Mary encouraging violence while in costume
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between Virgin Mary character and violent encouragement

jokes.text: The pubic hair. It's out!
jokes.setup: Larry fighting with Joseph
jokes.punchline: The fight dislodging the hair that's been bothering him all episode
jokes.explanation: Perfect episode-ending callback that resolves the running gag through violence
